what_it_does: An analysis/consolidation-draft markdown produced by a consolidation-drafter subagent. It examines two coordination briefs it names as overlap members (build-prep/coordination/COGNITION-BRIEF.md and GUIDED-REVIEW-BRIEF.md) and concludes KEEP SEPARATE — BY DESIGN. The argument: these files are correspondence (signed, session-owned halves of a handshake), not documentation, so merging would orphan two sessions' signatures and erase the deltas the protocol depends on. It inventories what each brief uniquely holds, flags one staleness hazard, and gives a proposed (never-executed) disposition table.
observations: Opens with a do-not-consolidate verdict and frames the two files as correspondence between two live work-lanes, not two descriptions of one system.
Argues the 'duplicated system-context' is the protocol's error-detection mechanism — each session restating the split in its own words is how mutual understanding is verified.
Section 3 shows the agreement-substance already consolidated in the authoritative place (WORK-SPLIT.md), with a table mapping each agreement item to its location in both briefs and in WORK-SPLIT.md.
Cites the truth ladder (live gates > git history > claims board > memory) and the duplicated-ownership-memory fork hazard as the reason per-session restatement matters.

A unique-content inventory lists what each brief alone holds (cognition's ownership inventory + standing offers vs guided-review's fork-identity + 8-row files-touched map + three C consumer-requirements).

Surfaces one real issue: a staleness hazard inside COGNITION-BRIEF.md (a 'RESOLVED — one owner' section superseded by a later Tim CORRECTION declaring it genuinely three sessions), with a proposed one-line superseded-pointer fix owned by the cognition session.
Disposition table: both files keep-as-is, no consolidated file created, nothing retired.
standouts: The correspondence-not-documentation framing — these are signed handshake halves whose value is in the deltas between restatements, so merging would destroy authorship/provenance.

The observation that consolidation has ALREADY happened in the right place (WORK-SPLIT.md is the agreement single-source-of-truth) and a second merge would create a competing truth-surface below the board's own ratified ladder.
The staleness hazard (a reader of COGNITION-BRIEF alone gets the wrong final ownership state) caught precisely because two voices each described the same work as theirs.
conventions: Consolidation-draft convention: verdict → rationale → unique-content inventory → disposition table; proposed-not-executed, no source touched.
Treats exchange records like the foundation/exchanges archive (the conversation IS the construction — preserved verbatim, per-voice).
Subordinate-to-the-board framing (truth ladder; claims-board authoritative over memory).
Expansion-over-reduction / preserve-in-place; any edit attributed to the owning session's signature.
concerns: Self-flagged: COGNITION-BRIEF.md alone presents a superseded 'one owner' resolution as final, a real misleading-on-read hazard, mitigated only by the truth ladder unless the proposed pointer line is added.
References approximate line anchors in runtime/suite.py and dated WORK-SPLIT.md sections that may drift over time.
notes: If briefing someone: this is a no-execute analysis arguing two coordination briefs are signed correspondence between sessions and must stay separate, since the agreement-substance already lives consolidated in WORK-SPLIT.md — plus one staleness fix the cognition session should consider.
questions: Will the cognition session add the proposed superseded-pointer line, or rely on the truth ladder to mitigate the stale 'one owner' section?
Is the ownership resolution genuinely settled as three sessions with guided-review as forward-owner of surface/FE/wire?
Are the three C consumer-requirements in GUIDED-REVIEW-BRIEF still the active inputs the cognition build is consuming?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches. The header states it is a consolidation-draft, proposed-only, nothing executed; the body delivers a keep-separate verdict with rationale, unique-content inventory, one flagged issue, and a never-executed disposition.
apparent_themes: Restatement-as-verification (the deltas do real coordination work)
intention_signals: Trying to prevent a well-intentioned merge that would damage a live coordination protocol — proving the briefs are signed correspondence whose separation is load-bearing, while surfacing the one genuine staleness hazard for the owning session to fix.
novelty: The framing of inter-session briefs as a handshake whose redundancy is the error-detection mechanism — duplication that verifies mutual understanding rather than wasting space — is a distinctive justification for non-consolidation.
contribution: Contributes a file-local analysis of the two coordination briefs: the correspondence-not-documentation judgment, the per-brief unique-content inventory, and the flagged ownership-staleness hazard with a proposed pointer fix. A fused system would keep this as the recorded reasoning for why signed session correspondence is preserved per-voice.
summary_for_embedding: A no-execute consolidation-draft analyzing two coordination briefs (COGNITION-BRIEF and GUIDED-REVIEW-BRIEF), concluding keep-separate as signed inter-session correspondence whose agreement-substance already lives in WORK-SPLIT.md, plus one ownership-staleness flag.
intention_for_embedding: To stop a merge that would orphan session signatures and erase coordination deltas, by proving the briefs are correspondence and surfacing the one genuine staleness hazard.
